rulings.Case Title: VERNON FARQUHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 2, DEFENDANT SASCO ELECTRIC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. · The motion is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses are not factually devoid and Defendant failed to negate Plaintiff's testimony regarding Defendant's employees disturbing asbestos material at the Fairchild plant in 1982. · Triable issues of fact also exist based on the Declarations of Plaintiff, Ay and Cohen. · The objection to the hearsay statement in Plaintiff's declaration is sustained. The objections to the reliance of Cohen and Ay to the presence of "Sasco" at the site is overruled for that is a disputed fact but they are entitled to rely on Plaintiff's version. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: VERNON FARQUHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 2, DEFENDANT CRITCHFIELD MECHANICAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. · When examining Plaintiff's deposition testimony and Plaintiff's answers to the special interrogatories, it is the Court's conclusion that Defendant shifted the burden of proof in that Plaintiff's collective discovery responses do not reflect a basis, beyond Plaintiff's speculation, that the fireproofing material being removed contained asbestos. · Defendant's objections to the Cantley declaration are overruled and that declaration creates a triable issue of fact along with the Cohen declaration and the motion for summary judgment is denied. · Plaintiff did not rebut Defendant's evidence that Defendant was only a contractor and the motion for summary adjudication as to the products liability and 402-B false representation causes of action is granted. Since the burden of proof was shifted, Plaintiff was required to come forward with evidence supporting the punitive damages claim and has not done so. Accordingly, summary adjudication is granted as to that claim. · Summary adjudication is denied as to the negligence claim based on triable issues of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JULIUS STEWARD et al VS. STEVEN C VILLARREAL II et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 4, DEFENDANT STEELE INTERNATIONAL, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion to vacate the default and the related discovery orders and to quash the writ of execution is granted on the conditions stated based on CCP § 437(b). The conduct of counsel is responsible for the default. The motion is granted on the condition that the moving party respond to all oustanding discovery within thirty days of the date this order is signed and if plaintiff is required to file a motion to compel any further response to the discovery and the motion is granted in any material respect, this order to vacate shall be vacated upon the request of plaintiff. In addition, plaintiff shall pay attorney's fees and costs of $14,847.84. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JANET PICKENS-STANFORD VS. DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Defts To: 1) Quash Defective Service Of Process; 2) Declare Pltf A Vexatious Litigant; 3) Prohibit Pltf From Filing New Actions Wihtout Leave Of Presiding Judge; And 4) Require Pltf To Furnish Security; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 7, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND LAURELLE PERRY'S Motion Of Specially Appearing Defendants To: 1) Quash Defective Service Of Process; 2) Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant; 3) Prohibit Plaintiff From Filing New Actions Without Leave Of Presiding Judge; And 4) Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security; Request For Judicial Notice. · The motion to quash service of summons is granted. Ms. Perry has not been personally served nor served by substituted service. Plaintiff has not served either the Board of Supervisors or the Mayor's Office. The Health Services is not an entity to be served. · Plaintiff is declared a vexatious litigant under CCP § 391(b)(1). Measured from the time the motion for security is filed, Plaintiff has had more than five cases determined against her in the past seven years. Stolz v. Bank of America (1993) 15Cal.App.4th 217. · Hearing required to determine if Plaintiff can demonstrate the ability to meet the requirements of filing a government claim. Absent doing so, Plaintiff is required to furnish security in the amount of $500 to be filed with the Clerk of the Court within 30 days. · The request to preclude Plaintiff from filing further suits is denied without prejudice to be brought before the Presiding Judge. =(302/PJM/AY)
